verdict: 2026-07-15 08:58 PDT — I would keep the signed, raw-body-verified `/auth/deauthorize` route and URL-validation handshake, but I would not build the compliance-callback half. The spec is based on a retired Zoom workflow and must be corrected before merge.

findings.title: The design is centered on Zoom’s deprecated Data Compliance API
findings.claim: Zoom’s current documentation says the Data Compliance API is deprecated and `user_data_retention` was removed; Zoom’s platform announcement directs developers to discontinue using it. Current deauthorization guidance requires receiving the signed event and deleting associated user data, but does not require a compliance callback. Consequently, `app_deauthorized` is coupled to an obsolete endpoint: an inoperative/non-2xx response becomes 500 and invites repeated webhook delivery instead of completing deauthorization. The outdated requirement originates in AC3 and must be revised, not preserved merely because the implementation matches it. Sources: [Data Compliance API](https://developers.zoom.us/docs/api/rest/data-compliance/), [Zoom deprecation announcement](https://developers.zoom.us/docs/platform/announcements/), [current deauthorization guidance](https://developers.zoom.us/docs/integrations/end-user-auth/).
findings.alternative: Retain signature verification, replay-window enforcement, and `endpoint.url_validation`. For a valid `app_deauthorized`, perform the documented no-op purge and immediately acknowledge with 200/204. Delete `COMPLIANCE_URL`, `CALLBACK_TIMEOUT_MS`, `complianceBody`, Basic-auth construction, fetch/timeout/error handling, OAuth-credential gating, corresponding callback tests, and callback documentation; update AC3 accordingly.
findings.win: Eliminates an obsolete external call and its retry/failure path, removes the deauthorization route’s unnecessary dependency on OAuth credentials and fetch, and deletes roughly 50 production lines plus several callback-specific tests while preserving the actual Marketplace requirement.
